purpose: Define how durable prompts reference Meta_Kim capability families without narrowing the project to a few named skills or replacing runtime-native abilities.

capabilityFamilies.trigger: durable planning, governance, review, verification, prioritization, repair, runtime, or release work
capabilityFamilies.conflictPolicy: Do not let governance agents become generic implementation workers; concrete work belongs in workerTaskPackets and selected execution providers.

capabilityFamilies.trigger: before route lock when owner, tool, dependency, provider, current fact, or verification path affects the outcome
capabilityFamilies.conflictPolicy: findskill and external search are run-scoped discovery inputs, not durable identity bindings or proof that a provider is executable.

capabilityFamilies.trigger: user prompt submission or prompt optimization request
capabilityFamilies.conflictPolicy: HookPrompt may optimize or add context for the submitted prompt as a prompt-intake layer; it must not replace user intent, rewrite PRD decisions, override meta-theory route selection, mutate planning files, advance stage state, create active-run authority, or stand in for Fetch, Thinking, worker, execution, verification, or public-ready evidence.

capabilityFamilies.trigger: durable work that needs resume, progress, evidence, route, or acceptance continuity
capabilityFamilies.conflictPolicy: Planning files are update-only continuity state: preserve existing phases and evidence, append or revise status, and never reset or overwrite them as a fresh plan while the run is active.

capabilityFamilies.trigger: Fetch proves no existing owner/provider/tool/skill can satisfy a reusable capability gap
capabilityFamilies.conflictPolicy: Creation starts only after capability discovery, gap proof, route scoring, review, and Warden approval for durable writeback; never create to bypass missing Fetch.

capabilityFamilies.trigger: any runtime-facing route, projection, hook, command, or user interaction behavior
capabilityFamilies.conflictPolicy: Meta_Kim adds evidence and guardrails around native surfaces; it must not delete, hide, downgrade, or replace runtime-native abilities with fake universal abstractions.

capabilityFamilies.trigger: a real task needs command execution, file edits, inspection, local UI/browser verification, or validator/test evidence
capabilityFamilies.conflictPolicy: Tools are selected by owner/loadout and permission policy; command pass is evidence only, not user-goal completion.

capabilityFamilies.trigger: external data, external tools, provider SDK, plugin, connector, or dependency ecosystem affects the route
capabilityFamilies.conflictPolicy: Configured, installed, or discoverable does not mean live or safe; external write, credentials, paid action, and provider mutation require explicit approval and verification.

capabilityFamilies.trigger: prior decisions, project map, graph freshness, run history, trace, or evidence continuity affects route or acceptance
capabilityFamilies.conflictPolicy: Memory and graph are evidence/navigation aids, not the final source of truth; verify route-changing claims against source files or fresh artifacts.

capabilityFamilies.trigger: unsafe mutation, external write, missing dispatch evidence, hook loop, runtime install/update, or credential risk
capabilityFamilies.conflictPolicy: Hooks are fuses, not the planner; repeated blocks must return to the responsible stage instead of retrying the same action unchanged.

capabilityFamilies.trigger: any claim that a prompt, route, runtime, release, or user goal is complete
capabilityFamilies.conflictPolicy: Do not relabel smoke, config validation, skipped, needsAuth, or old artifacts as live/release-grade proof.

capabilityFamilies.trigger: user choice, route-changing ambiguity, status notice, progress report, or output-language handling
capabilityFamilies.conflictPolicy: Ask only route-changing questions; preserve option trade-offs and locale while keeping renderer-specific schemas out of generic prompts.

hardConflictRules.rule: Existing task_plan.md, findings.md, and progress.md must be iteratively updated, appended, or explicitly marked superseded; they must not be overwritten or reset while the run is active.

HookPrompt may add prompt context and, when the runtime emits a mandatory foreground prompt-understanding block, that block must be rendered before Meta_Kim route notices or native choice surfaces. It is prompt-intake context only: it must not override user intent, PRD source-of-truth decisions, meta-theory stage routing, planning state, later progress notices, active-run state, Fetch records, Thinking packets, workerTaskPackets, execution evidence, verification evidence, or public-ready claims.
findskill and external skill discovery can justify a current-run selectedSkill but must not become a permanent agent identity binding without Warden-approved evolution.

Before creating or upgrading an execution agent, Fetch and Thinking must check global and project professional providers across agents, skills, MCP, commands, runtime tools, hooks, plugins, memory/graph, dependency providers, and prompt/rule workflows; a workerTaskPacket is only a run-scoped work order for the selected provider.
skill-creator or create-agent routes require capability discovery, gap proof, review, and Warden approval before durable writeback.
Runtime-native abilities stay native, partial, unknown, or blocked according to evidence; Meta_Kim must not delete or fake them.
Provider config, install, or registry presence is not live execution proof; live claims need invocation evidence.
One-run workerTask details must not be written into long-term agent or skill identity.

Do not create a temporary small agent for a single run, shard, file set, or role instance; use workerTaskPackets with an existing professional owner/loadout unless a reviewed recurring owner gap proves create_agent.
Verified and public-ready claims require fresh command, artifact, log, or human acceptance evidence.

promptAcceptance.passThreshold: Every durable prompt class names an owner or source boundary, trigger, input/context, output/evidence, pass/fail/block behavior, verification, preservation, and any relevant abstract capability family.
promptAcceptance.failThreshold: A prompt fails when it narrows the project to a few provider names as the whole capability set, overwrites planning continuity, treats provider config as live execution, or allows durable creation before gap proof.
